market_summary: The March 22, 2026 setup remains risk-off: recent AP/IBD coverage shows U.S. equities closing a fourth straight weekly decline, while Cboe shows VIX elevated at 26.78 as of March 20. Oil and rates remain the dominant macro inputs, with energy-linked inflation pressure and tighter financial conditions still capping broad risk appetite. Given your strategy history, the best edge is selective relative-strength and support/resistance execution, not broad momentum chasing.

CPB.reasoning: Unusual-volume flow in CPB aligns with your risk-off playbook favoring defensive cash-flow names over high-beta growth. The setup fits your strongest regime edges (relative strength and support/resistance) with clearer downside invalidation than broad index longs.
catalysts: Ongoing defensive rotation in a high-VIX tape
risks: If macro tone flips risk-on, defensive relative strength may fade quickly
Weekend/early-week headline reversals can reduce follow-through

XOM.reasoning: Energy remains a relative-strength pocket under persistent oil-risk narrative, but weekend headline sensitivity increases gap risk. Keep this as a conditional long rather than immediate size-up to avoid chasing an extended move.
risks: Cease-fire/de-escalation headlines can hit oil-linked equities quickly
Broad market liquidation can drag even leaders intraday

risks: High VIX can produce failed upside breaks

risks: Post-earnings gap fade in high-volatility tape
